top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 84, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Algarve Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
78ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Algarve Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 84 miles.
At Algarve Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 84 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Algarve Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
6,562ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 84 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Algarve Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,562ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: February in Lagoa, Algarve, Portugal. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Algarve Gran Fondo — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Algarve Gran Fondo’s 84 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Algarve Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Algarve Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 84 miles of mixed terrain in Lagoa, Algarve, Portugal.
